title: We need a back up plan to stay alive in case Trump wins

enrichment.summary: Kyle expresses enthusiasm for Bernie Sanders winning the presidency, then pivots to outlining his personal draft-dodging contingency plan in case Trump wins and starts a war with Iran. His escalating plan includes claiming conscientious objector status, taking a desk job, and as a last resort, getting peace sign tattoos and stripping naked in the field so the enemy will capture him and put him in a safe house.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Excited supporter, wants him as president
Fears it will lead to war with Iran
Would refuse to fight; prefers any nonviolent alternative over combat
Would not self-harm as a dodge — rules it out explicitly
Believes mass naked peace-tattoo protest could create unity and signal unwillingness to fight

transcript.text: Hello. I've been really excited lately about the future of America because of Bernie Sanders, how we're going to get a new president, whenever that is, October or something.
Yeah, he's going to tax the rich, and then we're going to get all of our social welfare programs, and we're all going to ignite the economy like a plane into utopia land.
But then, you know, I thought, well, what's the other side? If Trump gets in, if Trump gets in, he's going to probably attack Iran.
So, you know, we need to figure out a plan to stay alive. That's what my plan is. Stay alive, but not be a liar. You know what I mean?
I'd rather not lie ahead of staying alive. So I won't be like, I'm crazy, like Ted Nugent. But what I'll do is I'll be like, I'm pacifist religion.
But if that doesn't work, I guess I'll be like, give me a desk job. But if that doesn't work, I'm not going to injure myself.
You know, I guess I put myself in harm's way before I injure myself. This is just my thinking now.
And so, if they make me go, what I would do is I would get, I'd grow my hair up, like really long, and my beard out, really long.
And then I would get a giant peace sign tattooed on my, all over my chest and my back.
And I'd take all my clothes off.
And then when they throw me into the desert, I'd just take all, off all the gear that they make me put on.
And then I would just sit there like, like that.
Hey man, they made me come.
Like when the, whenever the enemy comes.
And then maybe the enemy could capture me and put me into like a safe house.
Until the war's over and then I come home.
Um, and then if a lot of people do that in mass, that would, you know, it would be like unity in numbers.
Because, um, because then they would know who the naked guys with the tattoo, the peace design tattoos on their bodies are.
And that they just don't want any trouble and that they had to go because they got drafted by Trump.
So, um, you know, I think it would be different for people to think about so that we could have the idea ready in case.
You know, it's always good to have a backup plan.
It's good to dream and be starry-eyed and, you know, be optimistic.
But it's also good to have your backup plan ready in case it just doesn't go that way.
Because, uh, you know, you never know what might happen.
All right, that was that.
